audio.summary: Maria walks through her morning routine, checking yesterday's sales and ordering produce before service.

steps.audio_excerpt: First thing I do is pull up yesterday's numbers.

analysis.action: Reviewing yesterday's sales by menu item
analysis.intent: Understand what sold so prep can be planned
step_by_step: Sorted the sales report by quantity sold

steps.audio_excerpt: Then I check what we're low on before I order.

analysis.action: Comparing top sellers against the inventory sheet
step_by_step: Cross-referenced best-selling dishes with stock counts
Highlighted ingredients below the reorder threshold

steps.audio_excerpt: I place the produce order and message the kitchen.

analysis.action: Placing a produce order and notifying kitchen staff
analysis.intent: Restock low ingredients and brief the team
step_by_step: Logged into the produce supplier portal
Added low-stock items to the cart
Sent the prep list to the kitchen WhatsApp group

workflow.title: Daily Opening Routine
workflow.trigger: Start of the business day, before lunch service prep begins
procedure: Open the POS dashboard and review yesterday's sales by item
Compare top-selling dishes against the inventory spreadsheet
Identify ingredients below the reorder threshold
Log into the supplier portal and add low-stock items to the cart
Send the prep list to the kitchen staff chat
decision_points: If an ingredient is above its reorder threshold, skip it from the order
If a top seller is at risk of selling out, flag it as a menu availability concern
exceptions: Supplier portal is down — fall back to phoning the order in
An item is out of stock at the supplier — pick a substitute or alert the chef
suggested_automations: Sales Summary Agent — pulls and summarizes yesterday's POS sales
Inventory Gap Agent — flags ingredients below threshold
Supplier Ordering Agent — drafts the produce cart for approval
Staff Briefing Agent — posts the prep list to the kitchen chat

clarifying_questions.question: How do you decide an ingredient is low enough to reorder?
clarifying_questions.why: Pins down the exact rule the Inventory Gap Agent should apply.

clarifying_questions.question: Should the supplier order be submitted automatically or wait for your approval?
clarifying_questions.why: Determines whether the Supplier Ordering Agent acts or only drafts.

clarifying_questions.question: Who on the kitchen team needs the prep list, and on which channel?
clarifying_questions.why: Defines where the Staff Briefing Agent should post.
